message:
chore(beads): record backward_create_graph silent gradient swallow bug (frankentorch-aba)

Add frankentorch-aba ("backward_create_graph silently swallows
tensor read errors as empty gradients", priority 2, type bug,
status in_progress).

Diagnosis (verbatim from issue body):
TensorTape::backward_create_graph in
crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs around lines 12933 and 12958 calls
`grad_node.tensor.contiguous_values_as_f64().unwrap_or_default()`
in two places. If the inner call returns an error (e.g.
DenseTensorError::UnsupportedLayout for a non-contiguous result,
or StorageSpanOverflow for a malformed meta),
unwrap_or_default() yields an empty Vec<f64>, and the leaf's
gradient silently becomes [].

Two distinct downstream risks:

1. TensorBackwardReport reports a None / empty-but-nominally-success
   gradient. Callers can't distinguish "no contribution" from
   "the read errored and we swallowed it".

2. The persistent_grads accumulator path zip-iterates over an
   empty `vals`, leaving the entry untouched on second-and-later
   backward passes. Gradient accumulation across multiple
   .backward() calls silently degrades into a no-op for affected
   leaves, which can pass naive correctness tests (the first
   call succeeds and the comparator only checks magnitude on the
   first pass).

This violates the project's non-regression rule (autograd
correctness outranks kernel speed) by hiding a real
DenseTensorError as a missing gradient.

Fix path recorded in the issue:
- Propagate the error via `?` into AutogradError (which already
  has `From<DenseTensorError>`), so a malformed gradient tensor
  surfaces at the backward boundary instead of being silently
  dropped.

No source change in this commit; the implementation lands in a
follow-up commit on crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankentorch-aba","title":"backward_create_graph silently swallows tensor read errors as empty gradients","description":"TensorTape::backward_create_graph (crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs ~12933, ~12958) calls grad_node.tensor.contiguous_values_as_f64().unwrap_or_default() in two places.

If contiguous_values_as_f64() returns an error (e.g. UnsupportedLayout for a non-contiguous result, or StorageSpanOverflow for a malformed meta), unwrap_or_default() yields an empty Vec, and the leaf's gradient silently becomes [].

Risks:
- The TensorBackwardReport reports a None/empty-but-nominally-success gradient.
- persistent_grads accumulator zip-iterates over an empty 'vals', leaving the entry untouched on second-and-later backward passes — silently breaking gradient accumulation across calls.

This violates the project's non-regression rule (autograd correctness outranks kernel speed) by hiding a DenseTensorError as a missing gradient.

Fix: propagate the error via ? into AutogradError (already has From<DenseTensorError>), so a malformed gradient tensor surfaces at the backward boundary instead of being silently dropped.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:41:46.841448116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:41:51.632249617Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-abw","title":"Align AGENTS workspace/oracle notes with actual FrankenTorch checkout","description":"AGENTS.md still shows a workspace tree that omits ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data, and it references a legacy_pytorch_code checkout that is not present in this repository snapshot. Update the project-description sections so future agents get an accurate repo map without changing the behavioral doctrine.","notes":"Updated AGENTS.md so future agents see rch-based verification commands, the real 12-crate workspace, the optional nature of the local legacy oracle mirror, and the higher-level ft-nn/ft-optim/ft-data stack in both architecture and testing sections.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:31:44.420818527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:32:32.326167181Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T17:32:32.325559041Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-aca","title":"Add messages to bare unreachable!() calls (5 sites)","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found 26 `unreachable!()` calls across the codebase. 21 already have explanatory messages or surrounding comments. 5 do NOT have messages, making debugging harder if they ever fire due to future code changes.

## Bare unreachable!() sites to fix
These are the sites that lack an explanatory message string:

1. ft-dispatch/src/lib.rs — multiple bare unreachable!() in dispatch routing match arms
2. ft-autograd/src/lib.rs:2947 — bare unreachable!() in backward pass routing

The remaining 21 sites already have messages like `unreachable!("complex dtypes rejected above")` and need no changes.

## Plan
1. For each bare `unreachable!()`, add a message string explaining WHY it is unreachable, e.g.:
   `unreachable!("all dispatch key variants handled above")`
2. Do NOT change logic — only add the message parameter
3. Verify with clippy that no new warnings are introduced

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 26 unreachable!() calls across the workspace have explanatory message strings
- Zero logic changes
- Clippy clean

## Reasoning
If the "impossible" state ever becomes possible due to a new enum variant or refactor, the message tells the developer exactly which invariant was violated. This is a defense-in-depth measure aligned with the DAC correctness doctrine.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:22:46.119557624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:39:23.927388620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:39:23.927143606Z","close_reason":"Added explanatory messages to all remaining bare unreachable!() sites across crates and verified workspace check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-alm","title":"Implement functional API: normalize, dropout, embedding, linear","description":"Add session-level functional ops matching torch.nn.functional: normalize (Lp normalization along dim), dropout (random zeroing), embedding (lookup table), linear (y=xA^T+b). These are commonly used in custom model forward() methods outside of nn.Module.","notes":"Verified that ft-api already implements the requested session-level functional ops: functional_normalize, functional_dropout, functional_embedding, and functional_linear. Confirmed with targeted rch-offloaded tests: cargo test -p ft-api functional_ -- --nocapture (10 passed).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T19:20:50.949752813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T19:23:33.532023017Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T19:23:33.531077685Z","close_reason":"Implemented functional_normalize (Lp/dim), functional_dropout (inverted scaling), functional_embedding (lookup with 2D indices), functional_linear (matmul+bias). 10 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
